panels.description: Membership step execution counts by status
panels.title: Membership Steps Rate

panels.description: Membership step duration percentiles
panels.title: Membership Duration (p95)

panels.description: Number of connected peer admins
panels.title: Successful Peer Connections (Total)

panels.description: Peer connection attempts by result
panels.title: Discovery Rate (Scans / Peer Connections / DNS)

panels.description: Metadata recomputation duration percentiles
panels.title: Metadata Recomputation Duration

panels.description: Current cluster assignment state
panels.title: Assigned Clusters

panels.description: Number of orphaned clusters detected
panels.title: Orphaned Clusters

panels.description: Admin cluster degraded status (1 = degraded)
panels.title: Admin Cluster Status

panels.description: Proxy connection rate by protocol and result
panels.title: Proxy Connection Rate

panels.description: Proxy session duration percentiles (p50, p95, p99) across all protocols, routing modes, and clusters. Short sessions (<1s) indicate connection failures or misconfigured clients. Long sessions (>5m) are healthy persistent tunnels.
panels.title: Proxy Session Duration (p50/p95/p99)

panels.description: Breakdown of successful proxy connections by routing mode. local = connection handled on this admin (Gateway owns the cluster, zero-copy socket transfer). remote = connection routed cross-admin via Erlang distribution. High remote % means clients are hitting the wrong admin for their cluster.
panels.title: Proxy Routing Mode Split (local vs remote, direct vs chain)

panels.description: Proxy connection rate broken down by cluster. Identifies which clusters are generating proxy load.
panels.title: Proxy Connection Rate by Cluster

panels.description: Rate of tunnels closing, split by reason. 'normal' is clean EOF from either side. 'deadline' means the total-duration cap fired (possible slowloris). 'drain_timeout' means the graceful-drain grace window expired on shutdown.
panels.title: Proxy Tunnel Close Rate (by reason)

panels.description: Proxy throughput per second, split by direction (up = client→target, down = target→client) and protocol.
panels.title: Proxy Throughput (Bytes/sec, by protocol + direction)

panels.description: Tunnel forwarding duration (time spent actually moving bytes, excluding handshake). p95 spikes indicate long-lived tunnels or stuck connections. Compare with session duration to see handshake overhead.
panels.title: Proxy Tunnel Duration (p50/p95/p99)

panels.description: Health check duration percentiles
panels.title: Health Check Duration

panels.description: Number of unhealthy or unreachable nodes in last health check run
panels.title: Unhealthy / Unreachable Nodes (Last Run)

panels.description: Health check results by status
panels.title: Health Check Rate by Result

panels.description: Command execution lifecycle rates
panels.title: Command Execution Lifecycle

panels.description: Command execution duration percentiles
panels.title: Command Execution Duration (sent → result received)

panels.description: Quantum job execution rate by job name and result
panels.title: Quantum Job Execution Rate

panels.description: Quantum job duration percentiles
panels.title: Quantum Job Duration (p95)

panels.description: Quantum job exceptions by job name
panels.title: Quantum Job Exception Rate

panels.description: Zombie admin cleanup runs by result
panels.title: Zombie Admin Cleanup Rate

panels.description: Number of zombie admins deleted in last cleanup
panels.title: Zombies Deleted (Last Run)

panels.description: Execution delivery runs by result
panels.title: Command Delivery Rate

panels.description: Number of executions delivered in last run
panels.title: Executions Delivered (Last Run)

panels.description: Gateway connection events (connects and disconnects)
panels.title: Gateway Connection Events

panels.description: Current number of active gateway connections
panels.title: Active Gateway Connections

panels.description: Gateway metrics scrape operations by cluster, type, and result
panels.title: Gateway Scrape Rate

panels.description: SSH credential verification attempts per second, broken down by result (success/failure) and auth method (password/public_key)
panels.title: SSH Verification Rate

panels.description: Total SSH verification failures. Non-zero sustained rate may indicate a brute-force attempt or misconfigured client.
panels.title: SSH Failures (Window)

panels.description: Cluster reconciliation run rate by result. Each run syncs one cluster's DB state against Netmaker. Errors trigger Oban retries.
panels.title: Cluster Reconciliation Rate

panels.description: Cluster reconciliation duration percentiles. Spikes indicate Netmaker API latency or large clusters with many nodes to sync.
panels.title: Cluster Reconciliation Duration (p50/p95)

panels.description: Self-update request completion rate by targeting type. Shows how often update requests are processed and what targeting strategy is used.
panels.title: Self-Update Requests Completed

panels.description: Nodes triggered vs failed in last self-update request. High failed count means agents unreachable or self_update_enabled=false.
panels.title: Self-Update Nodes Triggered vs Failed (Last Request)

panels.description: Broker publish rate by result (ok vs error). Sustained error rate indicates broker connectivity or auth issues — Oban will retry failed publishes.
panels.title: Event Broker Publish Rate (by result)

panels.description: Broker publish latency percentiles. High p95/p99 may indicate broker back-pressure; if the broker is remote, also reflects network latency.
panels.title: Event Broker Publish Duration (p50/p95/p99)

panels.description: Delta between enqueue rate and successful publish rate. A sustained positive value means events are piling up in Oban faster than the broker is accepting them — broker is degraded.
panels.title: Enqueue vs Publish Gap (broker backlog signal)
